subject: Ecology
title: The Carbon Cycle
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Carbon Cycle usable.

hook.text: Carbon atoms drift from the atmosphere into leaves, into animals, and back to the air.

buildup.text: Photosynthesis pulls CO₂ out of the air; respiration and decay put it back.

discovery.text: Oceans absorb about a quarter of human-made CO₂, slowing atmospheric buildup.

twist.text: Fossil fuels are ancient carbon buried for millions of years. We released it in decades.

climax.text: The cycle still works—but humans have overloaded one side, warming the planet.

punchline.text: Same carbon, faster loop, bigger problem.

quiz.question: How have humans disrupted the carbon cycle?
options: By burning fossil fuels and releasing ancient carbon
